Petition For Instructions And For Order Authorizing Retention Of Counsel To Defend Estate In Ongoing Litigation (Pc 9611)
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Myers for and with Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett.
Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett.
Curtis McBride (Sibling) also present in Propria Persona.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding whether or not there is a conflict of interest with Mr. Myers representation of his Clients as the Administrators and as individuals. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court granted the Petition for Instructions and for Order Authorizing Retention of Counsel to Defend Estate in Ongoing Litigation in so far as that the Administrators may proceed to engage litigation Counsel with the understanding that the Estate litigation Counsel can not represent them as Individuals and subject to the Court's approval of the terms of that engagement (subject to receipt and review of a revised order). Mr. Vann's objections to hiring an Attorney are all overruled. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest or Order Directing Sale of Estate Property is off calendar without prejudice to it being re-noticed if and when appropriate.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Reporter: Ken Brill, CSR #12797, 415-274-9977, sfdepo@veritext.com

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Additional Bond
Off Calendar Jul-24-2019 Continued To Aug-07-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Myers for Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett.

Mr. Myers stated that he has the verification from back in June to request authority from full authority to limited authority to lower bond to $10,000.00, and that he thought he had filed back then and realized he hadn't. The Court noted that an additional bond was ordered in the amount of $105,000.00, that the bond should have been in place during the time the assets were being administration, that is hasn't been posted and asks Counsel how he intends to fix this. Mr. Myers stated that they will post bond and then seek a refund for the time that is is not applicable. Status on filing of bond is continued to August 7, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The order to appear is off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Status on filing of the additional bond is also continued to that date.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-23-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Myers for Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett.
Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett.
Curtis McBride (Son of Decedent) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Myers stated that the house is worth $800,000.00 or $900,000.00, that there are litigation fees of $100,000.00 and that he spent time in litigation. Mr. Myers also stated that the opposition attempted a partition action, that mediation was unsuccessful, that at first there was a claim for adverse possession, that it appears they had to abandon that claim, that now there is a claim of an oral life estate and that there is a demand for a jury trial in the Civil Court. Mr. Vann stated that his Client had an agreement with the Siblings that he would have a life estate in the property provided he paid the taxes, the insurance and maintain up keep of the property, that his Client has done it for twenty five years and that in 2017 at the sale hearing he informed the parties that he was going to amend the complaint. The Court questions whether the estate needs to be involved in the litigation. Mr. Myers stated that the estate holds title to the property. Mr. Vann stated that they did not sue Mr. McBride, that Mr. McBride did not breach the agreement, that they sued against two of the individuals for breach of agreement, that the petition to sell the property is stayed and that he sued them as individuals. Mr. Myers stated that estate has a stake in Kenneth's life estate interest, that the estate owns the property, and that he can provide further briefing with respect to the issue that the estate has an interest during Kenneth's life estate and after his death. Mr. McBride provided history, and spoke of meetings and conversations that took place between the Siblings. The Court encouraged the parties to contact the Court's ADR/Judicial Mediation Department. The First Account, etc. is under submission. The Petition for Partition on calendar for July 2, 2019 is continued to August 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Partition Of Undivided Interest Or Order Directing Sale Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Oct-02-2018 Continued To Apr-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lily Moallem for Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett.
Dennis Vann for Kenneth Garrett.

The Court noted that Judge Quidachay overruled the demurrer and indicated in his view that it is appropriate to move forward in the civil action. Ms. Moallem stated that her only concern is that the estate needs to be administered and that there is no dispute that the asset belongs to the estate. Mr. Vann stated that the next hearing in the civil matter is set for October 23, 2018. The Court stayed the probate matter pending the outcome of the civil matter. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest, etc. is continued to April 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on progress of the civil matter. The Parties are to file a joint status statement by April 4, 2019. Ms. Moallem is to file a petition for approval of report on status of administration no later than October 11, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Partition Of Undivided Interest Or Order Directing Sale Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Sep-18-2018 Continued To Oct-02-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lily Moallem for Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett (Co-Administrators).
Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett.

Mr. Vann stated that he has not received a ruling on the demurrer in the civil matter. Ms. Moallem stated that she checked this morning and the demurrer is still under submission, that the asset is still subject to probate administration and that she will be filing a status report shortly. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest or Order Directing Sale of Estate Property is continued to October 2, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the civil case ruling and for filing of a petition for approval of report on status of administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Partition Of Undivided Interest Or Order Directing Sale Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Aug-14-2018 Continued To Sep-18-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett.
Pete Ryan for Co-Administrators.

Mr. Ryan stated that they have accepted service of the civil case, that he has filed his demurrer which is to be heard on September 6, 2018 in Dept. 501 and raised the issue of the case being heard in the Probate Department. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest, etc. is continued to September 18, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Partition Of Undivided Interest Or Order Directing Sale Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Jul-10-2018 Continued To Aug-14-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Pete Ryan for Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett (Administrators)
Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett

Mr. Ryan stated the matter is not settled, and the civil case has not been served even though it was filed prior to his petition being filed. Mr. Vann stated Mr. Ryan had previously stated he would accept service, that he sent it 6 days ago, and he did not act on it since they were in mediation. Mr. Ryan stated his client advised him not to acknowledge service, that Mr. Garrett doesn't have title to property, the title is in the decedent's name. The Court noted it cannot move forward until it can determine if the civil case or probate case has priority with respect to the property. Mr. Ryan stated he will acknowledge service in a day or two, then file response/demurrer. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest, etc. is continued to August 14, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the court to make a decision after the civil case has been served to proceed on this case or stay this case in favor of the civil case.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For Partition Of Undivided Interest Or Order Directing Sale Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Jan-23-2018 Continued To Mar-20-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lily Moallem for Gwendolyn McCray and Donald Garrett.
Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett.

The Court noted that an Order to Show Cause for failure to serve is set in the Civil matter and questions why it has not been served. Mr. Vann stated that it is going to be amended and suggested mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 13, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 15, 2018. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on March 20, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Petition For Partition Of Undivided Interest Or Order Directing Sale Of Estate Property
Off Calendar Oct-10-2017 Continued To Dec-19-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Kenneth Garrett.
Lily Moallem and Pete Ryan for and with Gwendolyn McGray and Donald Garrett.

Mr. Vann stated that he wants to proceed with the civil case, and that it has not been served because they thought they were going to amend the complaint, but they are not going to. Mr. Ryan stated that they will move to stay that case. Ms. Moallem stated that it's a matter of a title dispute and it could be consolidated with this petition as an 850, that it would be more cost effective and that regardless, the title petition should be heard first. Mr. Ryan requested a 60 to 90-day continuance stating that he would be happy to receive notice on the complaint and that he will need to demurrer. The Petition for Partition of Undivided Interest, etc. is continued to December 19, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to talk about a litigation plan.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
